demos.text: First, the browser issues a GET request to http://localhost:3000, which the Rails application duly receives. The Rails router then maps the root route to the index action of ArticlesController. Thereupon, the index action uses the Article model to retrieve every article stored in the database. Rails automatically renders app/views/articles/index.html.erb, evaluating its ERB code to produce HTML. At last, the server sends a response containing that HTML back to the browser.
demos.reasoning: The passage uses highly structured, step-by-step narration with unusually formal transitional words such as “duly,” “Thereupon,” and “At last.” Its polished explanatory style and comprehensive description of a standard Rails request cycle are characteristic of AI-generated instructional text.

demos.text: Versions of Flask older than 0.11 used to have different ways to start the application. In short, the flask command did not exist, and neither did python -m flask. In that case you have two options: either upgrade to newer Flask versions or have a look at /server to see the alternative method for running a server.
demos.reasoning: The passage has a practical documentation style, with specific version details and command examples. Its wording is somewhat conversational and slightly awkward (“have a look at /server”), which is more consistent with human-authored technical text than highly polished AI-generated prose.

fields.description: True for AI-generated text; otherwise False
